name: MDTraj (Modern MD Trajectory Analysis)

performance.notes: Cython-optimized; fast for typical protein trajectories

agent_readiness.notes: Modern MD trajectory analysis library. import mdtraj as md. traj = md.load('trajectory.xtc', top='topology.pdb'). traj.n_frames. traj.n_atoms. traj.xyz. traj.time. sel = traj.topology.select('backbone'). traj.slice(sel). traj.superpose(traj, 0). rmsds = md.rmsd(traj, traj, 0). rmsf = md.rmsf(traj, 0). contacts = md.compute_contacts(traj, scheme='ca'). dssp = md.compute_dssp(traj). phi, psi = md.compute_phi(traj). Formats: XTC, DCD, NetCDF, TRR, HDF5, LAMMPS.

gotchas: LGPL-2.1 — dynamic linking required for commercial use
Topology required for XTC/DCD files (no coordinates without topology)
Maintenance has slowed vs MDAnalysis (less active development)
No type annotations — poor IDE support
best_when: RMSD, RMSF, contact analysis of protein MD trajectories
Secondary structure (DSSP) and dihedral angle analysis
When fast, clean API is preferred over MDAnalysis's verbosity
GROMACS XTC trajectory analysis with topology
avoid_when: Complex selection algebra (MDAnalysis is more powerful)
When active development/community is important (MDAnalysis is more active)
summary: MDTraj provides modern MD trajectory analysis with clean API. md.load('traj.xtc', top='protein.pdb'). md.rmsd(), md.rmsf(), md.compute_contacts(), md.compute_dssp(). topology.select('backbone'). Dihedral angles. LGPL-2.1 by Robert McGibbon/Stanford. Faster and cleaner API than MDAnalysis for typical analyses. Less actively developed. XTC/DCD require separate topology file.
